quranicRelevance: Very frequent. Covers: physical killing (Qatl), fighting or battling in war (Qital), the condemnation of murderers, and metaphorical expressions of cursing or severe trial ('qutila al-insanu ma akfarahu').

keyDerivations.meaning: To fight, wage war, or battle

figurativeOrRareUsages: قُتِلَ: Cursed or condemned (as in 74:19 'qutila kayfa qaddara').
قَتَلَهُ (Form I): To render weak, dilute wine with water, or subdue.
discardedNoise: Diluting wine with water until it is weak.
Descriptions of specific styles of combat.
summaryEn: The root centers on killing and fighting, defining the literal acts of combat (Qital) and homicide, as well as the figurative expression of divine condemnation/curse.
summaryAr: يتمركز هذا الجذر حول القتل والقتال، محدداً الأفعال المادية للقتال والقتل، وكذلك التعبيرات المجازية التي تفيد اللعن والهلاك.
rawText: اِسْتَقْتَلَ 10 [properly He sought, or courted, slaughter;] i. q. اِسْتَمَاتَ; (S, K;) meaning he cared not for death, by reason of his courage; (JM;) he resigned and subjected himself to slaughter, and cared not for death. (Mgh.) تَقَتَّلَتْ لَهُ 5 means تَخَضَّعَتْ لَهُ وَتَذَلَّلَتْ حَتَّى عَشِقَهَا. (A.) قَاتَلَهُ 3 He fought, or combated, him; contended with him in fight or conflict or battle. قَاتَلَ عَلَى دِيِن ٱللّٰهِ: see 3 in art. ازى. قَاتِلٌ Deadly; applied to a tree; (K in art. خمط;) and to poison. (TA in that art.) قَتَلَ الشَّىْءَ 1 , inf. n. قَتْلٌ, † He knew the thing; he was, or became, acquainted with it: (Msb:) [or rather, i. q.] قَتَلَهُ عِلْمًا, (Bd in iv. 156, and TA,) and خُبْرًا, (K,) and بِعِلْمِهِ, (Bd, ubi supra,) he knew it (Bd, K, TA) completely, (TA,) or thoroughly, very well, or superlatively well; as also نَحَرَهُ عِلْمًا. (Bd.) See أَثْبَتَ الشَّىْء مَعْرِفَةً in art. ثبت. قَتَّالٌ [Murderous; slaughterous; very deadly.] You say حَيَّةٌ قَتَّالَهٌ [A very deadly serpent]. (TA in art. اصل.) قَتَّلَ 2 : see a verse cited in art. عتب, conj. 4. قَتْلٌ : from this word is formed the pl. قُتُولٌ, on the authority of hearsay. (El-Jurjánee, in Msb, art. قصد.) مَقْتَلٌ A [vital] place in a man [or an animal, i. e.] where a wound causes death; (S, Msb;) as the temple: (Msb:) pl. مَقَاتِلُ. (S.) مُسْتَقْتِلٌ : see مُسْتَمِيتٌ. وَلِنِّى مَقَاتِلَكَ means حَوِّلْ إِلَىَّ وَجْهَكَ. (A.)
